ErrorTypeParse))\n182\t}\n183\t\n184\t// ValidationError represents errors that occur during YAML validation.\n185\t//\n186\t// ValidationError wraps validation failures and provides detailed\n187\t// YAMLErrorType implements YAMLError interface.\n211\tfunc (ve *ValidationError) YAMLErrorType() ErrorType {\n212\t\t// If Type is explicitly set, use it\n213\t\tif ve.Type != \"\" {\n214\t\t\treturn ve.Type\n215\t\t}\n216\t\t// Try to determine error type from error code or message\n217\t\tif ve.ErrorCode == ErrCodeRequiredField || (ve.FieldPath != \"\" && containsRequiredFieldKeywords(ve.Message)) {\n218\t\t\treturn ErrorTypeFieldNotFound\n219\t\t}\n220\t\tif ve.ErrorCode == ErrCodeConstraintViolation || containsConstraintKeywords(ve.Message) {\n221\t\t\treturn ErrorTypeConstraint\n222\t\t}\n223\t\treturn ErrorTypeValidation\n224\t}\n225\t\n226\t// Context implements YAMLError interface.\n227\tfunc (ve *ValidationError) Context() string {\n228\t\treturn ve.ContextStr\n229\t}\n230\t\n231\t// Error implements the error interface.\n232\tfunc (ve *ValidationError) Error() string {\n233\t\tif ve.Line > 0 {\n234\t\t\tif ve.FieldPath != \"\" {\n235\t\t\t\tif ve.Constraint != \"\" {\n236\t\t\t\t\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"validation error in %s at line %d, field %s: %s (constraint: %s)\", ve.FilePath, ve.Line, ve.FieldPath, ve.Message, ve.Constraint)\n237\t\t\t\t}\n238\t\t\t\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"validation error in %s at line %d, field %s: %s\", ve.FilePath, ve.Line, ve.FieldPath, ve.Message)\n239\t\t\t}\n240\t\t\tif ve.Constraint != \"\" {\n241\t\t\t\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"validation error in %s at line %d: %s (constraint: %s)\", ve.FilePath, ve.Line, ve.Message, ve.Constraint)\n242\t\t\t}\n243\t\t\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"validation error in %s at line %d: %s\", ve.FilePath, ve.Line, ve.Message)\n244\t\t}\n245\t\tif ve.FieldPath != \"\" {\n246\t\t\tif ve.Constraint != \"\" {\n247\t\t\t\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"validation error in %s at field %s: %s (constraint: %s)\", ve.FilePath, ve.FieldPath, ve.Message, ve.Constraint)\n248\t\t\t}\n249\t\t\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"validation error in %s at field %s: %s\", ve.FilePath, ve.FieldPath, ve.Message)\n250\t\t}\n251\t\tif ve.Constraint != \"\" {\n252\t\t\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"validation error in %s: %s (constraint: %s)\", ve.FilePath, ve.Message, ve.Constraint)\n253\t\t}\n254\t\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"validation error in %s: %s\", ve.FilePath, ve.Message)\n255\t}\n256\t\n257\t// Unwrap returns the underlying error for error wrapping chains.\n258\tfunc (ve *ValidationError) Unwrap() error {\n259\t\treturn ve.Err\n260\t}\n261\t\n262\t// String returns a formatted error message with context.\n263\tfunc (ve *ValidationError) String() string {\n264\t\tvar sb strings.Builder\n265\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"  Error: %s\\n\", ve.Message))\n266\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"  Type: %s\\n\", ve.YAMLErrorType()))\n267\t\tif ve.Line > 0 {\n268\t\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"  Location: Line %d\", ve.Line))\n269\t\t\tif ve.Column > 0 {\n270\t\t\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\", Column %d\", ve.Column))\n271\t\t\t}\n272\t\t\tsb.WriteString(\"\\n\")\n273\t\t}\n274\t\tif ve.FieldPath != \"\" {\n275\t\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"  Field: %s\\n\", ve.FieldPath))\n276\t\t}\n277\t\tif ve.Constraint != \"\" {\n278\t\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"  Constraint: %s\\n\", ve.Constraint))\n279\t\t}\n280\t\tif ve.ContextStr != \"\" {\n281\t\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"  Context: %s\\n\", ve.ContextStr))\n282\t\t}\n283\t\treturn sb.String()\n284\t}\n285\t\n286\t// NewValidationError creates a new ValidationError with the given parameters.\n287\t//\n288\t// This constructor function provides a convenient way to create properly initialized\n289\t// ValidationError instances with message, path, constraint, and error code.\n290\t//\n291\t// Parameters:\n292\t//   - filePath: Path to the file being validated\n293\t//   - message: Human-readable error message\n294\t//   - fieldPath: Dot-notation path to the invalid field (optional)\n295\t//   - constraint: Constraint that was violated (optional)\n296\t//   - code: Error code for programmatic handling (use empty string for default)\n297\t//\n298\t// Returns a properly initialized ValidationError that implements the YAMLError interface.\n299\t//\n300\t// Example usage:\n301\t//\n302\t//\terr := NewValidationError(\"config.yaml\", \"invalid port number\", \"server.port\", \"must be between 1-65535\", ErrCodeInvalidValue)\n303\tfunc NewValidationError(filePath string, message string, fieldPath string, constraint string, code ErrorCode) *ValidationError {\n304\t\t// Use provided code or default to generic validation error\n305\t\terrorCode := code\n306\t\tif errorCode == \"\" {\n307\t\t\terrorCode = ErrCodeValidationFailed\n308\t\t}\n309\t\n310\t\treturn &ValidationError{\n311\t\t\tFilePath:   filePath,\n312\t\t\tMessage:    message,\n313\t\t\tFieldPath:  fieldPath,\n314\t\t\tConstraint: constraint,\n315\t\t\tErrorCode:  errorCode,\n316\t\t\tType:       ErrorTypeValidation,\n317\t\t}\n318\t}\n319\t\n320\t// IsValidationError checks if an error is a ValidationError.\n321\tfunc IsValidationError(err error) bool {\n322\t\tvar ve *ValidationError\n323\t\treturn err != nil && (ve != nil || isYAMLErrorOfType(err, ErrorTypeValidation))\n324\t}\n325\t\n326\t// containsRequiredFieldKeywords checks if the message suggests a required field error.\n327\tfunc containsRequiredFieldKeywords(msg string) bool {\n328\t\tkeywords := []string{\"required\", \"missing\", \"not found\", \"must be provided\"}\n329\t\tlower := strings.ToLower(msg)\n330\t\tfor _, kw := range keywords {\n331\t\t\tif strings.Contains(lower, kw) {\n332\t\t\t\treturn true\n333\t\t\t}\n334\t\t}\n335\t\treturn false\n336\t}\n337\t\n338\t// containsConstraintKeywords checks if the message suggests a constraint violation.\n339\tfunc containsConstraintKeywords(msg string) bool {\n340\t\tkeywords := []string{\"constraint\", \"range\", \"length\", \"pattern\", \"invalid value\"}\n341\t\tlower := strings.ToLower(msg)\n342\t\tfor _, kw := range keywords {\n343\t\t\tif strings.Contains(lower, kw) {\n344\t\t\t\treturn true\n345\t\t\t}\n346\t\t}\n347\t\treturn false\n348\t}\n349\t\n350\t// FileError represents errors that occur during file I/O operations.\n351\t//\n352\t// FileError wraps file I/O errors and provides detailed\n353\t// information about the file operation that failed.\n354\ttype FileError struct {\n355\t\tOp        string    // Operation that failed (read, write, etc.) - \"Op\" for backward compatibility\n356\t\tPath      string    // Path to the file\n357\t\tOperation string    // Operation that failed (read, write, etc.) - \"Operation\" for clarity\n358\t\tMessage   string    // Human-readable error message\n359\t\tErr       error     // Underlying error for error wrapping\n360\t\tErrorCode ErrorCode // Error code for programmatic handling\n361\t}\n362\t\n363\t// Code implements YAMLError interface.\n364\tfunc (fe *FileError) Code() ErrorCode {\n365\t\tif fe.ErrorCode != \"\" {\n366\t\t\treturn fe.ErrorCode\n367\t\t}\n368\t\treturn ErrCodeFileIOError\n369\t}\n370\t\n371\t// YAMLErrorType implements YAMLError interface.\n372\tfunc (fe *FileError) YAMLErrorType() ErrorType {\n373\t\treturn ErrorTypeFile\n374\t}\n375\t\n376\t// Context implements YAMLError interface.\n377\tfunc (fe *FileError) Context() string {\n378\t\top := fe.Operation\n379\t\tif op == \"\" && fe.Op != \"\" {\n380\t\t\top = fe.Op\n381\t\t}\n382\t\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"operation: %s, path: %s\", op, fe.Path)\n383\t}\n384\t\n385\t// Error implements the error interface.\n386\tfunc (fe *FileError) Error() string {\n387\t\top := fe.Operation\n388\t\tif op == \"\" && fe.Op != \"\" {\n389\t\t\top = fe.Op\n390\t\t}\n391\t\tif op != \"\" {\n392\t\t\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"file error during %s on %s: %s\", op, fe.Path, fe.Message)\n393\t\t}\n394\t\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"file error in %s: %s\", fe.Path, fe.Message)\n395\t}\n396\t\n397\t// Unwrap returns the underlying error for error wrapping chains.\n398\tfunc (fe *FileError) Unwrap() error {\n399\t\treturn fe.Err\n400\t}\n401\t\n402\t// IsFileError checks if an error is a FileError.\n403\tfunc IsFileError(err error) bool {\n404\t\tvar fe *FileError\n405\t\treturn err != nil && (fe != nil || isYAMLErrorOfType(err, ErrorTypeFile))\n406\t}\n407\t\n408\t// SyntaxError represents YAML syntax errors during parsing.\n409\t//\n410\t// SyntaxError includes indentation errors, malformed YAML, and other\n411\t// syntax-related issues that prevent the YAML from being parsed.\n412\ttype SyntaxError struct {\n413\t\tFilePath  string    // Path to the file with syntax error\n414\t\tLine      int       // Line number where syntax error occurred\n415\t\tColumn    int       // Column number where syntax error occurred\n416\t\tMessage   string    // Description of the syntax  // Path to the file with duplicate keys\n639\t\tKey       string    // The duplicate key name\n640\t\tLocation  string    // Nested path to the duplicate key\n641\t\tLine1     int       // Line during\n// YAML file I/O, parsing, validation, and schema operations.\ntype YAMLError interface {\n\terror\n\n\t// Code returns the error code for programmatic error handling.\n\tCode() ErrorCode\n\n\t// YAMLErrorType returns the category of error for type switching.\n\tYAMLErrorType() ErrorType\n\n\t// Context returns additional context about the error.\n\tContext() string\n}\n\n// ErrorType = \"empty\"          // Empty content\n\tErrorTypeIO             ErrorType = \"io\"             // I/O error\n)\n\n// ErrorCode represents specific error codes for programmatic error        // Invalid schema definition\n)\n\n// ParseError represents errors that occur during YAML parsing.\n//\n// ParseError wraps underlying parsing errors and provides detailed\n// location {\n\tkeywords := []string{\"required\", \"missing\", \"not found\", \"must be provided\"}\n\tlower := strings.ToLower(msg)\n\tfor _, kw := range keywords {\n\t\tif strings.Contains(lower, kw) {\n\t\t\treturn true\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\treturn false\n}\n\n// containsConstraintKeywords checks if the message suggests a constraint violation.\nfunc containsConstraintKeywords(msg string) bool {\n\tkeywords := []string{\"constraint\", \"range\", \"length\", \"pattern\", \"invalid value\"}\n\tlower := strings.ToLower(msg)\n\tfor _, kw := range keywords {\n\t\tif strings.Contains(lower, kw) {\n\t\t\treturn true\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\treturn false\n}\n\n// FileError represents errors that occur during file I/O operations.\n//\n// FileError wraps file I/O errors and provides detailed\n//
